symptoms.symptom_name: Pain out of proportion to injury, refractory to strong opioids

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Disproportionate, opioid-refractory pain is the earliest and most sensitive feature of compartment ischaemia.
Ordinary fracture pain generally responds to appropriate opioid analgesia and is not this disproportionate.

symptoms.symptom_name: Excruciating pain on passive stretch (moving the toes)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pain on passive stretch of the muscles in the affected compartment is a hallmark clinical sign.

symptoms.symptom_name: Paraesthesia over the dorsum of the foot

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Early ischaemia of the deep peroneal nerve produces dorsal-foot paraesthesia — a time-critical progression sign.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tight, shiny, swollen, pale limb above the cast

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Visible tension, swelling and pallor over a swelling compartment constricted by a rigid cast; video/carer-observable so admissible in telehealth, unverified.

symptoms.symptom_name: Early paresis — weakness of toe movement

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Motor weakness is a later, ominous sign of advancing muscle/nerve ischaemia and a narrowing treatment window.

symptoms.symptom_name: Onset within hours of a rigid cast applied for a tibial fracture

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Closed tibial fracture plus a circumferential rigid cast is the classic precipitating setup for lower-leg compartment syndrome.

symptoms.symptom_name: Delayed capillary refill in the toes (approximately 4 seconds)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reduced distal perfusion; a clinician-observed sign, not reliably obtainable via chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Distal pulses (dorsalis pedis) remain weakly palpable

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pulses persist until the very late stage of compartment syndrome — their presence must NOT be used to exclude it.
Primary arterial occlusion typically loses distal pulses immediately, so palpable pulses argue against it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intracompartmental pressure exceeding 30 mmHg by needle manometry

investigation_details.expected_result: Anterior compartment pressure > 30 mmHg (elevated; delta pressure narrowed)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without emergency measurement and decompression the diagnosis is confirmed too late to save the muscle.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Directly measured elevated compartment pressure confirms the diagnosis — a hospital investigation, never available via telehealth.
Sympathetic response to severe pain and distress; clinician-measured vitals, non-specific and not obtainable without a device.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Disproportionate opioid-refractory pain, pain on passive stretch, and early paraesthesia together constitute a limb-threatening emergency after a rigid cast until decompression is achieved.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency orthopaedic assessment with intracompartmental pressure measurement
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms compartment syndrome and guides emergency fasciotomy; performed in the ED, not via telehealth. Escalation must not wait for it.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Repeat / increased oral opioid analgesia as a management step
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Chasing opioid-refractory pain with more analgesia masks the cardinal red flag and dangerously delays decompression — an error of commission.
